title: Redis OOM: maxmemory Too Low
description:
## The Situation

Your API service uses Redis as a caching layer. Since this morning's deploy, every endpoint that touches the cache is returning 500 errors. The on-call alert is firing.

Redis is running. The service is running. But every `SET` command fails with:

```
(error) OOM command not allowed when used memory > 'maxmemory'
```

## Your Task

1. Confirm the failure with `redis-cli`
2. Identify the broken configuration value
3. Fix it and apply the change
4. Verify writes succeed

## Useful Commands

```bash
# Test a write — this is currently failing
redis-cli set testkey testvalue

# Inspect memory usage and config
redis-cli info memory
cat /etc/redis/redis.conf | grep maxmemory

# Apply a live config change (no restart needed)
redis-cli config set maxmemory 256mb

# Or edit the file and restart
nano /etc/redis/redis.conf
service redis-server restart

# Run the app's cache test
python3 /home/user/cache_test.py
```
hints: Redis is returning an OOM error, but the server itself has plenty of memory. The limit is self-imposed — look at how Redis is configured.
Check /etc/redis/redis.conf for the maxmemory setting. Also check the maxmemory-policy — together they define what Redis does when it's 'full'.
Set maxmemory to a sane value (256mb or higher) in /etc/redis/redis.conf, then apply with: redis-cli config set maxmemory 256mb (live, no restart) or service redis-server restart.
